reverseString:
open System

let originalString = "F# Functional"

// Method 1: Using F# core functions (idiomatic)
// Convert string to list of chars, reverse list, convert back
let reversedList =
    originalString
    |> List.ofSeq // Convert string (sequence of chars) to list
    |> List.rev   // Reverse the list
    |> List.toArray // Convert back to char array
    |> String     // Construct string from char array
printfn "Original: %s" originalString
printfn "Reversed (F# list): %s" reversedList

// Method 2: Using .NET Array functions (imperative style)
let chars = originalString.ToCharArray() // Get .NET char array
Array.Reverse(chars)                    // Reverse array in-place
let reversedNet = String(chars)         // Create new string
printfn "Reversed (.NET array): %s" reversedNet

// Note: Both methods handle Unicode correctly.
primeCheck:
open System

// Function to check if a number is prime
let isPrime (n: int64) = // Use int64 for larger range
    if n <= 1L then false // Primes must be > 1
    elif n <= 3L then true // 2 and 3 are prime
    elif n % 2L = 0L || n % 3L = 0L then false // Divisible by 2 or 3
    else
        let limit = int64 (sqrt (float n)) // Calculate integer square root limit
        let rec check i =
            if i > limit then true // Checked all divisors up to limit
            elif n % i = 0L || n % (i + 2L) = 0L then false // Divisor found
            else check (i + 6L) // Check next pair (optimisation)

        check 5L // Start checking from 5

// Example usage
let numbersToCheck = [2L; 3L; 4L; 17L; 25L; 29L; 97L; 100L; 1L; -5L; 1000000007L]

numbersToCheck
|> List.iter (fun n -> // Iterate through the list
    if isPrime n then
        printfn "%d is prime." n
    else
        printfn "%d is not prime." n
)
writeFile:
open System.IO

let writeToFile (filePath: string) (lines: string seq) =
    printfn "--- Writing to '%s' (overwrite) ---" filePath
    try
        // WriteAllLines overwrites or creates the file
        File.WriteAllLines(filePath, lines)
        printfn "Successfully wrote %d lines." (Seq.length lines)
    with
    | ex -> printfn "Error writing to '%s': %s" filePath ex.Message

let appendToFile (filePath: string) (lines: string seq) =
    printfn "--- Appending to '%s' ---" filePath
    try
        // AppendAllLines adds to the end or creates the file
        File.AppendAllLines(filePath, lines)
        printfn "Successfully appended %d lines." (Seq.length lines)
    with
    | ex -> printfn "Error appending to '%s': %s" filePath ex.Message

// Example Usage
let outputFileName = "output_fsharp.txt"
let initialLines = [ "First line from F#."; "Second line." ]
let linesToAppend = [ "Third line, appended."; "Fourth line." ]

// Write initial content
writeToFile outputFileName initialLines

// Append more content
appendToFile outputFileName linesToAppend

// Display final content (by reading it back)
printfn "\\n--- Final content of '%s': ---" outputFileName
if File.Exists(outputFileName) then
    try
        File.ReadAllLines(outputFileName) |> Array.iter printfn // Print each line
    with
    | ex -> printfn "Error reading back file: %s" ex.Message
else
    printfn "File '%s' not found for display." outputFileName

// Optional: Clean up
// if File.Exists(outputFileName) then File.Delete(outputFileName)
fileExists:
open System.IO

// Function to check file/directory existence and type
let checkPath (path: string) =
    printfn "Checking: '%s'" path
    match (File.Exists(path), Directory.Exists(path)) with
    | (true, false) -> printfn " -> Exists as a FILE."
    | (false, true) -> printfn " -> Exists as a DIRECTORY."
    | (false, false) -> printfn " -> Does NOT exist."
    | (true, true) -> printfn " -> ERROR: Path exists as both file and directory? (Shouldn't happen)" // Should be rare/impossible

// Example Usage
let fileToCheck = "fsharp_exists_test.fs"
let dirToCheck = "MyFSharpFolder"
let nonExistent = "NoSuchThingHere.fxt"

// Create dummy file/dir
try
    File.WriteAllText(fileToCheck, "// F# test file") // Create empty file
    Directory.CreateDirectory(dirToCheck) |> ignore // Create directory
    printfn "Created dummy file '%s' and directory '%s'" fileToCheck dirToCheck

    printfn "\\n--- Checking Paths ---"
    checkPath fileToCheck
    checkPath dirToCheck
    checkPath nonExistent

finally // Ensure cleanup
    if File.Exists(fileToCheck) then File.Delete(fileToCheck)
    if Directory.Exists(dirToCheck) then Directory.Delete(dirToCheck) // Delete empty dir
    printfn "\\nCleaned up dummy file and directory."
dateTime:
open System

let showDateTimeInfo () =
    printfn "--- Current Date and Time ---"
    let now = DateTime.Now // Gets local time
    let utcNow = DateTime.UtcNow // Gets UTC time

    printfn "Local DateTime Object: %A" now
    printfn "UTC DateTime Object: %A" utcNow

    printfn "\\n--- Common Formats (.NET ToString) ---"
    // See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/base-types/standard-date-and-time-format-strings
    printfn "Short Date (d): %s" (now.ToString("d"))
    printfn "Long Date (D): %s" (now.ToString("D"))
    printfn "Short Time (t): %s" (now.ToString("t"))
    printfn "Long Time (T): %s" (now.ToString("T"))
    printfn "Sortable (s): %s" (now.ToString("s")) // ISO 8601 style
    printfn "RFC1123 (R): %s" (now.ToString("R"))
    printfn "Universal Sortable (u): %s" (utcNow.ToString("u")) // UTC sortable

    printfn "\\n--- Custom Formats ---"
    printfn "YYYY-MM-DD: %s" (now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd"))
    printfn "HH:mm:ss (24hr): %s" (now.ToString("HH:mm:ss"))
    printfn "Readable: %s" (now.ToString("dddd, MMMM dd, yyyy 'at' hh:mm:ss tt"))

    printfn "\\n--- Components ---"
    printfn "Year: %d" now.Year
    printfn "Month: %d" now.Month
    printfn "Day: %d" now.Day
    printfn "Hour: %d" now.Hour
    printfn "Minute: %d" now.Minute
    printfn "Second: %d" now.Second
    printfn "Millisecond: %d" now.Millisecond
    printfn "DayOfWeek: %A" now.DayOfWeek

    printfn "\\n--- Unix Epoch Seconds ---"
    // Calculate seconds since 1970-01-01 00:00:00Z
    let epoch = DateTime(1970, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, DateTimeKind.Utc)
    let epochSeconds = (utcNow - epoch).TotalSeconds
    printfn "Seconds since epoch (UTC): %f" epochSeconds // Result is double
    printfn "Seconds since epoch (int): %d" (int64 epochSeconds)


// Run the function
showDateTimeInfo()
maxInArray:
// Finding the maximum element in a list, array, or sequence

// Example List
let numbersList = [10; 5; 42; 17; 8; 99; 32; -5; 0]
printfn "List: %A" numbersList

// Use List.max (requires list not be empty)
if not (List.isEmpty numbersList) then
    let maxValList = List.max numbersList
    printfn "Maximum value (List.max): %d" maxValList
else
    printfn "List is empty."

// Example Array
let numbersArray = [|10.5; 5.1; 42.0; 17.3; 8.8; 99.9; 32.2; -5.0; 0.0|]
printfn "\\nArray: %A" numbersArray

// Use Array.max (requires array not be empty)
if numbersArray.Length > 0 then
    let maxValArray = Array.max numbersArray
    printfn "Maximum value (Array.max): %f" maxValArray
else
    printfn "Array is empty."

// Example Sequence (potentially lazy)
let numbersSeq = seq { 1 .. 2 .. 20 } // 1, 3, 5, ..., 19
printfn "\\nSequence: %A" (Seq.toList numbersSeq) // Realize sequence for printing

// Use Seq.max (requires sequence not be empty)
// Note: Seq.max iterates through the sequence
if not (Seq.isEmpty numbersSeq) then
    let maxValSeq = Seq.max numbersSeq
    printfn "Maximum value (Seq.max): %d" maxValSeq
else
    printfn "Sequence is empty."

// Handling empty collections more robustly using Option type:
let findMaxOption list =
    if List.isEmpty list then None
    else Some (List.max list)

match findMaxOption numbersList with
| Some maxVal -> printfn "\\nMax value using Option: %d" maxVal
| None -> printfn "\\nList was empty (using Option)."

match findMaxOption [] with // Test empty list
| Some maxVal -> printfn "Max value using Option: %d" maxVal // Won't print
| None -> printfn "List was empty (using Option)."
